etymology.description: Depicts four parallel lines representing the number four
etymology.interpretation: 亖 (sǐ, 'four lines') is a straightforward ideogram that consists of four parallel lines to visually represent the concept of four. It fell out of use after the Qin dynasty due to writing standardization in favor of 四 (sì, 'four'), which avoided confusion with 二 (èr, 'two') and 三 (sān, 'three') in vertical writing contexts.
